title: Mission Vao
content:
{{Top|mprot|leg}} {{MultipleIssues|image|expand|citation}} {{Character |type=Galactic Republic |image=[[File:Mission_Vao_Rener.jpg]] |option1=3956 BBY |image2=[[File:Mission-comic.jpg]] |option2=3963 BBY |name=Mission Vao |homeworld=[[Taris/Legends|Taris]]''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' |birth=c. [[3970 BBY]] |death= |species=[[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] {{C|[[Rutian/Legends|Rutian]]}} |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]{{DB|character|missionvao|Vao, Mission}} |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |skin=[[Rutian/Legends|Blue]] |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings=[[Griff Vao]] |children= |affiliation=*[[Hidden Beks]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Hey, don't treat me like I'm a little girl. I ain't no kid—I'm fourteen years old.|Mission Vao|audio=MissionLittleGirl.flac|}} '''Mission Vao''' {{Pronounced|'mɪʃɪn 'veo}} was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Rutian/Legends|Rutian]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] who lived as a street urchin on the [[Ecumenopolis/Legends|ecumenopolis]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and the [[Jedi Civil War]], with her older brother, [[Griff Vao]]. In [[3963 BBY]], Mission helped out the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Zayne Carrick]] by disarming his attacker, [[Brejik]]. After Griff had left Taris with dancing girl [[Lena]], Mission became the closest friend of the exiled [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] [[Zaalbar]]. In [[3956 BBY]], Zaalbar was captured by [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] slavers in the [[Undercity sewers]] of Taris. Vao sought out the help of the amnesiac former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. After Revan and Vao freed Zaalbar, she gave him access to the upper level of the sewers and joined him in his [[Rescue of Bastila Shan|quest]] to rescue Jedi [[Bastila Shan]]. Following the [[destruction of Taris]], she joined the crew of the [[Ebon Hawk]] on its search for the automated shipyard [[Star Forge]]. During her travels with Revan, Mission reencountered Griff, who was captured by the indigenous [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] of the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. When freed, Griff asked her and Revan to acquire [[Tach/Legends|Tach glands]] from the planet [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] to create and synthesize [[Tarisian ale]]. After giving Griff the glands, he made a mistake with the ale and disappeared. Following the [[Battle of Rakata Prime]], Vao along with Revan and his other companions, were awarded the [[Cross of Glory]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] for their efforts. Afterwards, she and Zaalbar parted ways with Revan and started a business venture together. By [[3643 BBY]], [[Holostatue/Legends|holostatues]] of Vao and Revan's companions were part of [[Axial Park]] on the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]. ==Biography== ===Childhood=== {{Quote|I never knew my parents, my brother always looked out for me. He's the one who brought me to Taris. I was just a kid, only five. But I remember the trip – if you could call it that. We were stuffed inside a packing crate in a star freighter's cargo hold with just enough food and water to make the trip. Not exactly first class, you know?|Mission to Revan|}} The [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] Mission Vao was born sometime around [[3970 BBY]] and grew up as an orphan, having never known her parents, with just her elder brother [[Griff Vao]] to look after her. Griff was a scam artist, a gambler, and a drinker, always borrowing [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] for his schemes of easy riches which regularly failed, leaving him in debt and on the run. Mission was only five years old when they escaped to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Taris/Legends|Taris]], smuggling themselves inside a packing crate in a [[Freighter/Legends|star freighter's]] cargo hold, barely having enough supplies to make the trip. On Taris, Griff and Mission had to live in the [[Lower City]] due to the alien ban that restricted access to the [[Upper City]]. ===Meeting Zayne=== {{Quote|I'm Mission.''"
"''I'm Fool's Errand.|Mission and Zayne Carrick|[[Knights of the Old Republic 22|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 22]]}} Around [[3963 BBY]], her brother Griff was a member of one of the Lower City's swoop gangs known as the [[Hidden Beks]]. People were still reeling from the consequences of [[Padawan Massacre|the murders]] of the four [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawans]] that were to help rim-worlds such as Taris maintain law and order, when the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] invaded. [[Zayne Carrick]], who had been framed for the murders, came back to Taris to try to help the [[Taris Resistance|resistance]] against the invading Mandalorians, and found the Hidden Beks' secret hideout. Mission introduced herself to Zayne after he was through talking to [[Marn Hierogryph]], and developed a crush on him, despite her brother's warning to stay away from the accused murderer. She then told him that she "knew a secret" and brought Zayne to see her brother's pet, which she thought was what Griff was feeding in secret. While they were spying on him, Gryph found them and inadvertently gave away their position to Griff. Meanwhile, [[Brejik]] attempted to kill Zayne with a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]]. Zayne was struggling to disarm Brejik when Mission bit his leg, causing Brejik to drop the blade. When he did, Griff came up behind her and pulled her away from the fight. Brejik then pulled out a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] pistol, telling Zayne that he would tell everybody that he was killed "trying to escape". [[Del Moomo]] then appeared from behind and pinned Brejik to a wall before he was able to kill Zayne. Mission, Zayne, and Gryph then discovered that the "pet" was not a pet at all; inside the container where Griff dropped the food were the missing children of [[Sector Constable|Constable]] [[Noana Sowrs]]. After they got the two children out of there, they brought them back to the Hidden Beks' leader, [[Gadon Thek]]. Mission then helped feed the two children. While Gadon was enraged at Griff and Brejik for kidnapping the children, it gave the Hidden Beks an opportunity to open up negotiations with the "True" Resistance, and then eventually join them. Griff and Mission were regulars of [[Javyar's Cantina|Javyar's cantina]] when Griff met [[Lena]], a Twi'lek dancing girl, and started dating her. Lena, however, used to date Tarisian [[Noble/Legends|nobles]], and Griff could never give her the kind of lifestyle she was used to. Eventually, he had to leave Taris, escaping his debts once again, this time together with Lena, and he left his twelve-year-old sister behind. He told Mission that Lena didn't want his little sister tagging along, but promised he would return as soon as they earned enough credits, and that then they would all live like royalty. Believing she made her brother forget about her, Mission hated Lena, and thought of her as a "busty, credit-grubbing [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] rat." She had no doubt their relationship wouldn't last long. ===Encounter with Zaalbar=== [[File:MissionAndZaalbarAtJavyarCantina.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Mission and Zaalbar at Javyar's Cantina.]] {{Quote|With my street smarts and his muscle, we make a great team.|Mission about Zaalbar|}} After her brother Griff deserted her on Taris, Mission got by on her own, until one day she made friends with [[Zaalbar]], a [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] outcast from [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]]. Zaalbar was being hassled by members of the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]], a major Lower City swoop gang, when he attracted her attention. The sight of thugs picking on a lone Wookiee overwhelmed by a strange environment made her lose her temper, and she charged at them. She was smacked down by one of the Vulkars, but then the Wookiee got angry and yanked him a meter up off the ground, scaring the others away. Despite such unusual circumstances of their meeting—or perhaps ''because'' of them—Mission and Zaalbar stuck together from then on, looking out for each other in their own way. She nicknamed Zaalbar "Big Z". For two years they got by in the Lower City, never hearing anything from or about Griff, and often seeking adventure in dangerous spots like the sewers of the Undercity, where Gadon used to tell her not to go. Mission never asked Zaalbar too much about his past, because he didn't like to talk about it, and she accepted that, dismissing the matter with the conclusion that he was the strong, silent type. The Wookiee would still talk a bit about his homeworld, about the big [[Wroshyr tree/Legends|Wroshyr trees]], and how he had "seen paradise destroyed by the greed of outsiders," which though Mission didn't understand at first, referred to [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]] and their [[Slavery/Legends|slaving operations]] on Kashyyyk. ===Entanglement with Revan=== {{Main|Rescue of Bastila Shan}} {{Quote|Please, you have to help me! Nobody else is going to help me. Even the Beks won't help me.|Mission Vao to Revan|}} In [[3956 BBY]], near the end of the [[Jedi Civil War]], decorated war hero and legendary [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] [[Carth Onasi]] was stranded on Taris with an amnesiac [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] following the [[Attack on the Endar Spire|attack on the ''Endar Spire'']], a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[Warship/Legends|warship]] under the command of the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Bastila Shan]]. The entire war effort against the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] relied on her skill with [[Battle meditation/Legends|battle meditation]], making her the Republic's only hope, so as soon as possible Carth and Revan made for the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]], where her [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]] had crashed. They passed by [[Javyar's Cantina]] where they found an unusual pair—a Wookiee, and a fourteen-year-old Twi'lek girl who preferred speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]] rather than her people's [[Twi'leki/Legends|native language]]. Revan watched as Zaalbar scared away some Black Vulkars picking on Mission. Seeing that Revan was a newcomer in the Lower City, Mission provided him with information about the city, the swoop gangs, local [[Exchange]] boss [[Davik Kang]], and the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Calo Nord]], who had just walked out of the cantina, leaving a few corpses behind. She also spoke of the [[Hidden Beks]] as a more honest and reliable gang than the [[Black Vulkars/Legends|Black Vulkars]], which were now led by Brejik, and she mentioned that Davik had recently acquired a new ship for his smuggling operations, the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', which he supposedly kept in [[Davik Kang's estate|his estate]]. Mission and Zaalbar then left the cantina to seek more adventures in the Undercity. [[File:MissionNeedsHelp.png|thumb|right|295x295px|Mission meeting Revan in the Taris Undercity.]] At the [[Hidden Bek Base]], the leader of the gang [[Gadon Thek]] made a deal with Revan, agreeing to provide sponsorship in the [[Tarisian Season Opener]] in exchange for the retrieval of a prototype accelerator that was stolen from him by the Vulkars. Revan was to break into the Vulkar base, where the [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] upgrade was being kept, and Gadon suggested that Mission could be able to get him in the back way. He then gave Revan authorization papers needed to get past the Sith guard at the elevator to the Undercity in exchange for the Sith uniforms he had used before as a disguise, and promised to let Revan use the accelerator to win the race and get Bastila back, who was being offered as a prize by Brejik, in his game to take over the Lower City. Once off the gates of the [[Outcasts (Taris)|Outcast]] village of the Undercity, Revan ran into a very distressed Mission. She explained that her friend Zaalbar had been caught by [[Gamorrean/Legends|Gamorrean]] slavers in the sewers and agreed to show Revan a secret entrance into the Vulkar Base if he helped her free the captive Wookiee. Revan saved Zaalbar from the slavers, and as customary to his people, the Wookiee swore a [[Life debt/Legends|life debt]] to him. Mission was not about to part ways with the only family she had since her brother left, so she too joined Revan. They followed him aboard the ''Ebon Hawk'', which Revan stole from Davik, just in time to escape the [[destruction of Taris]]. Although Mission was shocked to see her homeworld destroyed before her eyes, she told Revan she would find a way to deal with it, and that whatever help she could offer him against [[Darth Malak]] and the Sith she would. ===Travels on the ''Ebon Hawk''=== {{Gamemechanics}} {{Quote|Please, Griff. Let this be the last time. No more cons. No more scams. No more schemes. That's all I ask.|Mission Vao to Griff|}} [[File:MissionT3M4-KotORCG.png|thumb|left|258x258px|Mission Vao repairs [[T3-M4]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]].]]After the destruction of Taris, Mission accompanied Revan and his companions aboard the ''Ebon Hawk'' and assisted Revan in his journey to destroy the Star Forge. During these travels with Revan, Mission encountered Lena, who explained what had happened after she and Griff left Taris. She had dumped Griff, leaving him on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], where he was working for [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Corporation]]. She also said that she had actually wanted Mission to come with them, and even offered to pay for her ticket, but Griff told Lena that Mission wouldn't leave Taris. In reality, Griff had lied to them both and even though Mission wouldn't believe Lena at first, she realized that she had been misguidedly idolizing her brother. Mission still questioned the validity of Lena's statements and when Revan took the ''Ebon Hawk'' for a landing on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] in search of the [[Star Map]], they found Griff had been taken prisoner by [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. In the enclave where he was being kept, a conversation ensued that allowed Mission to realize the truth in Lena's words. Even after his liberation, Griff didn't give up his half-baked business projects—his latest one, in which he tried to use [[Tach/Legends|tach]] glands collected by Revan to brew [[Tarisian ale]], also failed, and he also got himself into trouble with the [[Exchange]]. Mission was angry with Griff, especially after witnessing him continue his get-rich-quick schemes. Eventually she realized, though nobody was perfect, her brother was a little less perfect than most for he never learned from his own mistakes; therefore, instead of fruitlessly trying to "improve" her brother, she learned to accept him as he was. Mission was happy that her brother was alive, but now she didn't feel as though she owed him anything anymore. {{Endgame}} After escaping the [[Leviathan (Interdictor-class cruiser)|''Leviathan'']], Revan revealed his former identity to the crew. Though shaken, Mission found that he was now a completely different person, and therefore stood by him. She followed Revan to the end of his quest, even to the monstrous [[Star Forge]]. She was present at the celebration of the Star Forge's destruction and the Republic's victory over the Sith, where Revan's companions were proclaimed as saviors of the Republic and Revan was awarded the [[Cross of Glory]]. When Revan departed for the Unknown Regions, Mission did not accompany him.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' ===Life in peace=== After parting ways with Revan, Vao and her longtime Wookiee best friend Zaalbar started a business venture together. In [[3954 BBY]], Revan planned one and final adventure into unknown space. Canderous Ordo had assumed that the former crew of the ''Ebon Hawk'' would finally be reunited, but Revan only intended to bring a select few of his old companions. Although Ordo proposed Vao and Zaalbar because of their usefulness, Revan rejected the idea. By this time, the two friends had become focused on expanding their import/export business. Thus, Revan had no interest in interrupting their lives any further.''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' A [[Holostatue/Legends|holostatue]] of Vao was erected in the [[Leisure Garden]] section of the [[Axial Park]] in the [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]] on [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], along with the statues of Revan and his other companions in memory of their deeds.{{TORcite|type=faction|Republic|Bonus: Restoring the Statues|Corellia}} ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Kids are like that?? Listen, you … Oh, I get it. Okay, you got me.|Mission, catching herself in mid-rant after a falling out with Republic pilot Carth Onasi|}} [[File:Missionfulltaris.jpg|thumb|278x278px|Mission Vao.]] Despite her harsh upbringing and abandonment, Mission was a sunny and optimistic girl. She believed the best of people most of the time and was the first to rally to Revan's defense after he was revealed as the former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] in the [[Skirmish aboard the Leviathan|skirmish aboard the ''Leviathan'']], as she had been convinced by Revan's actions over the course of their mission that he was a true friend. She was initially at a loss for words, much like the rest of the crew of the ''Ebon Hawk but'' seeing how he didn't remember anything about being the Dark lord she concluded that it didn't really matter anymore, much to the frustration of the paranoid and distrustful Carth. As a child, Mission was very curious, spying on her brother and often playing with the personal effects of Gadon's [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]] [[Zaerdra]], who would get mad at her elder brother for not keeping Mission away from her stuff. One trait she shared with her brother Griff was smooth talking, and she was known to be forward with boys she considered attractive, such as [[Zayne Carrick]]. She had a strong sense of fairness, as evidenced by her anger with [[Wookiee Chieftain]] [[Chuundar]] and [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka]] and their treatment of the Wookiees, and by her loyalty to the [[Hidden Beks]] after [[Brejik]]'s betrayal. She was easily influenced, however, and not only by Revan. She would let [[Kandon Ark]] twist her mind and seed animosity between her and Gadon, and there was little that Revan could not talk her into. She was also courageous and impulsive, charging three Vulkars by herself to try and save Zaalbar, or mouthing off at – and trying to bite – armed Sith troopers. Her chief annoyance, however, was when people dismissed her on the basis of age. This earned her several arguments with Carth and Bastila – the former of whom Mission made up with, and the latter using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to trip her when she retorted to a crack about her age. She also constantly teased Zaalbar like a bratty little sister, suggesting he take a bath, brush his teeth, dye his hair, or even wear a suit. ===Relationships=== ====Carth Onasi==== {{Main|Carth Onasi}} {{Quote|I don't have to listen to you, Carth! You ain't my father – though you're sure old enough to be! So keep your lectures inside your withered old head, 'cause I don't need 'em!|Mission Vao, to Carth Onasi|}} Vao felt insulted by Onasi when he said that Taris was no place for her, even with Zaalbar to look out for her. In response, Vao snapped at Onasi and made disparaging comments about his age. After some time, Vao felt sorry for what she had said and apologized to him. Onasi told Vao that she was important and needed and the pair reconciled. When Revan's true identity was revealed, Onasi did not understand how she was still able to stand by Revan's side. ====Zaalbar==== {{Main|Zaalbar}} {{Quote|Anyway, I don't like Vulkars at the best of times. And when I saw them picking on this poor Wookiee – all alone on a strange planet, overwhelmed by the big city – I just lost it. I screamed out 'Leave him alone, you core-slimes!' and charged right at them. One of them saw me coming and slapped me so hard he just about knocked me cold.|Mission Vao to Revan|}} Zaalbar, who Vao affectionately called "Big Z," was Vao's best friend. The pair's chance meeting on Taris saved Zaalbar from a life of senseless violence. Vao was able to see that Zaalbar was more than a brute and the two quickly became best friends. Whenever the irresponsible Vao got in trouble, Zaalbar was always close by to help her out.{{DB|character|zaalbar|Zaalbar}} Despite their contrasts, their friendship benefited them both. Zaalbar knew that Mission would always have a plan for food and shelter, while Vao knew that Zaalbar would would always be there to watch her back.[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] ==Skills and abilities== [[File:Mission_concept.jpg|thumb|150px|Concept art of Mission Vao.|left]] {{Quote|He showed me how to slice into a computer's security system; how to get inside a locked building without the entrance codes, and how to spot a wealthy mark for a quick shell game.|Mission, about her brother Griff|}} After Mission joined Revan's fellowship, she had the chance to apply her knowledge and skills to different ends from what she was used to in the streets of Taris. Her fine sleight of hand gained Revan entrance to the [[Black Vulkar Base]] from the sewers of the [[Undercity (Taris)|Undercity]], for which she had picked the codes off the pocket of a Black Vulkar. She was a good [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] and was proficient with [[Stealth field generator/Legends|Stealth field generators]]. When the ''Leviathan'' captured the ''Ebon Hawk'', and Revan had to choose a member of the crew for a rescue mission, she volunteered her skills with security systems, claiming she could slice her way out of any security cell. She could speak Basic and understand [[Shyriiwook/Legends|Shyriiwook]] and [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]]. She could also play the card game [[Pazaak/Legends|Pazaak]]. ==Equipment== [[File:Mission_Vao_on_Taris.png|thumb|right|250px|Mission Vao stalking Gamorreans in the Taris Undercity with her vibroblade.]] With only few resources, Vao modified a basic [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]] into an exceptional and adaptable weapon which was comparable to her own skills. She also utilized a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]]. During her first encounter with Revan, the Twi'lek wore a black [[shirt]], gray pants, black gloves, an armband, and a [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]]. ==Behind the scenes== {{Quote|She tried to bite me through my armor! And you should hear what she said about my mother. Admiral Karath needs to teach her the proper respect for the Sith!|A Sith trooper, when Vao is selected to rescue the player from the Leviathan|}} [[File:Mission_concept_art.jpg|thumb|140px|Early concept art of Mission Vao.]] Mission Vao first appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', where she was voiced by [[Catherine Taber]]. Vao, along with Zaalbar, Carth Onasi, Canderous Ordo, and Bastila Shan, was inspired by [[James Ohlen]]'s work on roleplaying campaigns for [[West End Games]].{{InsiderCite|144|Gaming the Expanded Universe: Ten Years of Knights of the Old Republic|Gaming the Expanded Universe: Ten Years of ''Knights of the Old Republic''}} Early drafts of the game had Mission as a male Twi'lek rather than a female. Also, the concept art of Mission's face did not satisfy the art director, who was seeking more of a teenage appearance. A tribute is made to Mission in the [[Star Wars: Legacy|''Star Wars: Legacy'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic books]] with the characters [[Astraal Vao]] and [[Shado Vao]],{{JCFCite|url=posts/25502945|board=Literature|thread=Legacy #5: Broken, part 4 (of 6)|user=[[Jan Duursema|Jan_Duursema]]|date=2006-11-03|time=8:20 PM|archiveurl=https://archive.is/2DAoC}} The letters page of ''Star Wars: Legacy'' issue [[Legacy (2006) 12|twelve]] stated that Vao was a common Twi'lek name and Mission was not related to Astraal and Shado.[[Legacy (2006) 12|''Legacy'' (2006) 12]] {{C|letters section}} In a tweet, Catherine Taber stated that she believed that Vao was an ancestor of the characters [[Vette]] and [[Numa]], both of whom were Rutian Twi'leks also voiced by her.{{Twitter|cattaber|status/790741579224887296|[[Catherine Taber]]|quote=I believe Vette, Mission & Numa from #CloneWars & #StarWarsRebels are all related (they DO have similar voices(; ) #StarWars #Twileks #SWTOR|archivefile=File:Catherine Taber-Numa Tweet.png}} ===Alternate dark side choices=== {{Quote|No! I'm not going to just stand aside and do nothing! You'll... you'll just have to kill me. But I don't think you will.|Mission Vao to Revan if the player chooses the Dark Side ending on Lehon|}} Alternatively, to Mission's coming to terms with her brother's character and forgiving him, the player could convince her to embrace her feelings of anger and betrayal. She cuts all ties off with Griff and leaves him to whatever fate the Exchange has for him. While the ending where Revan is redeemed and the Republic triumphant is considered canonical, there is an alternate ending of the game where Bastila seduces Revan to the dark side of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] on [[Lehon/Legends|Lehon]]. When Revan and Bastila return to the ''Ebon Hawk'', Mission refuses to follow the self-proclaimed Dark Lord of the Sith. Mission didn't believe that Revan had truly fallen back to the dark side, and that if she didn't attack first, Revan wouldn't be able to bring himself to kill her in cold blood. [[File:Missiondead.jpg|thumb|Mission killed at the hands of Zaalbar.|left]] However, she had sorely underestimated the ruthless Sith Lord, and Revan commanded her best friend Zaalbar kill her for him instead. Naturally Zaalbar refused at first, but by manipulating him with the Force and his life-debt, Revan finally convinced him to slay the dumbstruck Mission with a single mighty blow. As if to signify that Revan's fate was now forever set down the dark path, Bastila applauds his callousness and comments that Revan is as dark now as Malak ever was. It is also possible to attack Mission without convincing Zaalbar to kill her. In this case, Zaalbar abandons his life debt to Revan and protects Mission. Both are killed at the hands of the newly reborn Darth Revan. ==Appearances== *[[Knights of the Old Republic 22|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 22]] *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} *''[[The Old Republic: Revan]]'' {{Ab|The Old Republic: Revan (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' {{Mo}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{Hologram}} *[[Cartel Market|''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' Cartel Market]] {{C|As outfit}} *''[[Star Wars: Galaxy of Heroes]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Mission Vao}} *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] *[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'': Prima Official Game Guide]] *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Legacy Era Campaign Guide]]'' *{{DB|character|missionvao|Vao, Mission}} *{{DB|character|zaalbar|Zaalbar}} *{{InsiderCite|130|My Star Wars|My ''Star Wars'': Catherine Taber}} *{{InsiderCite|144|Gaming the Expanded Universe: Ten Years of Knights of the Old Republic|Gaming the Expanded Universe: Ten Years of ''Knights of the Old Republic''|reprint=1}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{K1char}} {{Interlang |de=Mission Vao#legends |el=Μίσιον Βάο |es=Mission Vao |hu=Mission Vao |it=Mission Vao |no=Mission Vao |pl=Mission Vao |pt=Legends:Mission Vao |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Vao, Mission}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Hidden Beks]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Rutian Twi'leks]] [[Category:Tarisians]]
